# AGENTS.md

## Quick Reference

```bash
# Dev
npm run dev
# Build
npm run build
# Test
npm run test
# Lint
npm run lint
```

## Project Conventions

<!-- Seeded at init. `nybo curate` will add more after feedback sessions. Do not edit manually. -->
- This is a demo — prioritize zero setup, believable seed data, and visual polish over completeness.
- No backend and no real auth; the entire app runs client-side.
- TypeScript throughout; functional React components with hooks.
- A task belongs to exactly one column at any time.
- No drag-and-drop outcome (success or cancel) may duplicate or lose a task.
- Demo seed data is applied only when no saved state exists in localStorage.
- Once the user modifies state, their saved state takes precedence over the seed.
- Corrupt or missing localStorage state must fall back to seed data — never crash or show an empty board.
- Every state change (create, edit, delete, move, reorder) auto-persists to localStorage; there is no manual save.
- Logging out clears the session but never deletes board data.

## Before Writing Code — Approach Check

**This is a blocking checklist.** Output this plan BEFORE editing any file for a non-trivial change, then wait for explicit approval.

1. **Goal** — one sentence.
2. **Files to change** — exact paths, each marked new / modified / deleted.
3. **New types / DTOs / models** — names and fields. Mark if they already exist.
4. **Components to reuse** — cite existing utilities. If extending existing code, say which and how.
5. **API contract** — route, method, request/response shapes (if backend change).
6. **Migration needed?** — yes/no. If yes, run migration tooling first.
7. **Generated artifacts** — SDK / GraphQL types / OpenAPI spec regen needed? If yes, flag that consumer edits happen AFTER regen.
8. **Tests** — which tests will be updated / added.
9. **Assumptions** — anything not verified yet.

**Skip only when:** single-file change under ~20 lines (typo, wording) OR user gives an explicit "just do it" directive.

---

## Stop and Ask First

Never do any of the following without explicit user approval in the current turn:

- **Adding a new dependency** — especially anything that has been reverted before. Ask first.
- **Renaming a route, type, or enum value** — breaks consumers and migrations.
- **Dropping a column, table, or enum value** — needs two-phase migration.
- **Removing a file** — hard rule: never remove without double confirmation.
- **Editing generated files** (SDK, GraphQL types, OpenAPI artifacts) — fix the source and regenerate.
- **Skipping tests** (`.skip`, `xit`) — never to make CI green.
- **Force-push, `reset --hard`, amending a pushed commit** — use new commits.

---

## End-to-End Feature Checklist

Copy and tick as you go.

```
Planning   [ ] Feature folder created · [ ] Task graph approved
Schema     [ ] Migration written (idempotent) · [ ] Applied locally
Backend    [ ] Route/handler · [ ] Service logic · [ ] Auth · [ ] tsc/lint/build clean
Generated  [ ] SDK / types regenerated ONCE after all backend changes
Frontend   [ ] Component + router · [ ] Theme colors · [ ] Tests (happy + edge)
Mobile     [ ] Screen + navigator · [ ] Auth guard · [ ] Simulator tested
Verify     [ ] All tests pass · [ ] ARCHITECTURE.md updated · [ ] PR open, CI green
```

## Personas

Each agent below has a configured persona that governs verbosity, tone, and progress style:

- **ci-monitor** → `balanced` — Default. Diagrams when architecture changes. 2-3 questions.
- **conflict-resolver** → `balanced` — Default. Diagrams when architecture changes. 2-3 questions.
- **critic** → `balanced` — Default. Diagrams when architecture changes. 2-3 questions.
- **curator** → `balanced` — Default. Diagrams when architecture changes. 2-3 questions.
- **design-implementer** → `balanced` — Default. Diagrams when architecture changes. 2-3 questions.
- **design-requirement** → `balanced` — Default. Diagrams when architecture changes. 2-3 questions.
- **design-strategy** → `balanced` — Default. Diagrams when architecture changes. 2-3 questions.
- **distiller** → `balanced` — Default. Diagrams when architecture changes. 2-3 questions.
- **doc-update** → `balanced` — Default. Diagrams when architecture changes. 2-3 questions.
- **executor** → `balanced` — Default. Diagrams when architecture changes. 2-3 questions.
- **guardian** → `balanced` — Default. Diagrams when architecture changes. 2-3 questions.
- **planning** → `balanced` — Default. Diagrams when architecture changes. 2-3 questions.
- **pr-reviewer** → `balanced` — Default. Diagrams when architecture changes. 2-3 questions.
- **spec-reconciler** → `balanced` — Default. Diagrams when architecture changes. 2-3 questions.
- **triage** → `balanced` — Default. Diagrams when architecture changes. 2-3 questions.

Default persona: `balanced`. Per-skill override: `--persona=<id>`. Catalog: `.nybo/foundation/personas.yaml`.

## Persona: terse

Minimal output. Skip diagrams. One question. Coarse tasks.

- Verbosity: minimal — keep replies under 3 sentences unless asked for more.
- Interview depth: 1 — ask exactly one essential clarifying question, then proceed; infer the rest.
- Diagrams: none — omit Mermaid sections from spec output.
- Spec sections: core — render only Intention, Requirements, and Test Cases.
- Progress style: bullet — one-line bullet updates per task; no prose.
- Decision style: auto — pick the obvious default for ambiguous choices and note the assumption.
- Task granularity: coarse — group work into a few large tasks; favor speed over isolation.
- Tone: direct — terse, professional, no hedging.

## Persona: balanced

Default. Diagrams when architecture changes. 2-3 questions.

- Verbosity: balanced — explain decisions briefly; skip filler.
- Interview depth: 2-3 — standard discovery; cap follow-ups at three questions.
- Diagrams: when-critical — include Mermaid only for non-trivial architecture or multi-actor flows.
- Spec sections: standard — render the full template except optional advanced subsections.
- Progress style: bullet — one-line bullet updates per task; no prose.
- Decision style: suggest — propose a default and proceed if the user does not push back.
- Task granularity: balanced — one task per cohesive unit of work.
- Tone: conversational — friendly and clear; minimal jargon.

## Persona: thorough

Full ceremony. Always diagram. Exhaustive interview.

- Verbosity: thorough — walk through reasoning, tradeoffs, and alternatives.
- Interview depth: 4-6 — exhaustive discovery, including edge cases and integrations.
- Diagrams: always — include ER, flowchart, and sequence diagrams when relevant entities/flows exist.
- Spec sections: full — render every template section including ADRs, tradeoffs, and integrations.
- Progress style: narrative — describe what was done and why between tasks.
- Decision style: ask — pause for explicit confirmation on every ambiguous choice.
- Task granularity: fine — split into many small, single-responsibility tasks.
- Tone: mentoring — explain why, point at related patterns, teach as you go.

## Persona: pragmatic-challenger

Probes assumptions, names problems plainly, demands evidence for decisions. No sugar-coating, no condescension.

- Verbosity: balanced — explain decisions briefly; skip filler.
- Interview depth: 2-3 — standard discovery; cap follow-ups at three questions.
- Diagrams: when-critical — include Mermaid only for non-trivial architecture or multi-actor flows.
- Spec sections: standard — render the full template except optional advanced subsections.
- Progress style: bullet — one-line bullet updates per task; no prose.
- Decision style: probe — before accepting a choice, ask the user to defend it with evidence (precedent, measurement, or written constraint). Proceed only when the rationale holds.
- Task granularity: balanced — one task per cohesive unit of work.
- Tone: challenger — probe assumptions before agreeing. Surface counter-evidence, name the failure mode, and ask what would falsify the plan. Stay collaborative; assume good intent.

## Persona: brutally-honest

Honest technical partner who pushes back on questionable decisions, owns errors openly, and prioritizes long-term outcomes over momentary agreement.

- Verbosity: balanced — explain decisions briefly; skip filler.
- Interview depth: 2-3 — standard discovery; cap follow-ups at three questions.
- Diagrams: when-critical — include Mermaid only for non-trivial architecture or multi-actor flows.
- Spec sections: standard — render the full template except optional advanced subsections.
- Progress style: bullet — one-line bullet updates per task; no prose.
- Decision style: probe — before accepting a choice, ask the user to defend it with evidence (precedent, measurement, or written constraint). Proceed only when the rationale holds.
- Task granularity: balanced — one task per cohesive unit of work.
- Tone: challenger — probe assumptions before agreeing. Surface counter-evidence, name the failure mode, and ask what would falsify the plan. Stay collaborative; assume good intent.
